steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is A, $-7p + 16q - 8$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Set up the subtraction
steps.content:
Substitute the expressions for $a$ and $b$ into $a - b$ and distribute the negative sign carefully:
$$a - b = (4p + 7q) - (11p - 9q + 8)$$
$$= 4p + 7q - 11p + 9q - 8$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Group and combine:
$p$ terms: $4p - 11p = -7p$
$q$ terms: $7q + 9q = 16q$
constant: $-8$
So $a - b = -7p + 16q - 8$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $-7p + 16q - 8$: Matches our simplified expression exactly.
  • B) $-7p + 16q + 8$: Wrong sign on the constant; subtracting $+8$ gives $-8$, not $+8$.
  • C) $-7p - 2q + 8$: Wrong $q$ coefficient ($7q - (-9q) = 16q$, not $-2q$) and wrong constant.
  • D) $-7p - 2q - 8$: Wrong $q$ coefficient; sign of $-9q$ must flip to $+9q$.
A is correct.
